data.title: SEC Drops OpenSea Investigation Easing Pressure on NFT Market
data.description:
• The SEC is closing its investigation into OpenSea, according to the NFT marketplace’s founder and CEO.
  • The decision is seen as positive for the cryptocurrency industry and NFT creators and comes shortly after the SEC moved to end its lawsuit against Coinbase.

data.title: Ether Supply Squeeze? Bybit Hacker Emerges as World's 14th-Largest ETH Holder
data.description:
• Ether traded 2% higher as the hacked ETH is seen as a permanently lost supply.
  • Bybit may need to purchase ETH, one observer noted.

data.title: Crypto Exchanges Start to Fill Bybit's $1.4B Hole as Hackers Move Stolen Funds
data.description:
• Bybit stolen funds are on the move and being converted to bitcoin.
  • Bitget has moved some of its own reserves to Bybit to help support the exchange, CEO Gracy Chen said.
  • Chen added that users' funds are securely stored on the platform.

data.title: Arthur Hayes Proposes Rolling Back Ethereum Network to Negate $1.4B Bybit Hack
data.description:
• Ether holder Arthur Hayes raised the idea of an Ethereum rollback as solution to the Bybit hack.
  • Other market participants are worried that a potential rollback would break the Ethereum ecosystem.

data.title: Bybit CEO Labels Pi Network a Scam, Citing Official Police Warning
data.description:
• Bybit's CEO cited a 2023 Chinese police warning that Pi Network is a scam targeting elderly people.
  • The project's referral scheme and "token lock-up " offering are similar to crypto Ponzi schemes Bitconnect and Hex.
  • PI has dropped more than 60% from its launch-day peak.

data.title: North Korean Hackers Were Behind Crypto's Largest 'Left of All Time'
data.description:
• Arkham said the North Korean hackers Lazarus Group was behind the $1.5 billion hack, citing ZackXBT.
  • The attack appeared to have been caused by something called "Blind Signing."
  • The attackers first withdrew nearly $1.5 billion worth of funds from the exchange into a main wallet and then distributed them to several different wallets.

data.title: Plunging U.S. Stocks Help Add to Crypto's Bad Day
data.description:
• Early gains in cryptos and crypto-related stocks first took a hit from the $1.5 billion Bybit hack
  • A quick retreat in U.S. stocks in later in the day pressured crypto even further.
  • Among news items hitting stocks was a poor read on consumer sentiment and a report on a new coronavirus discovered in China.

data.title: CZ Suggests Bybit Halt Withdrawals, Offers Help With $1.5B Hack
data.description:
• Binance founder CZ has suggested Bybit freeze withdrawals following Friday's $1.5 billion hack.
  • "Will provide any assistance if needed," CZ added.
  • ZachXBT reported that two batches of 10,000 ETH has been split up across 48 wallets.

data.title: Costa Rica Makes First Foray Into Crypto with New Bitcoin ETF
data.description:
• Costa Rica’s largest commercial bank is launching a bitcoin ETF.
  • It’s the first crypto investment vehicle from Costa Rica’s banking sector.
  • The country doesn’t have any formal crypto legislation.

data.title: Bitcoin, Ether Slump as Crypto Prices Dip on Report of Massive $1.4B Bybit Hack
data.description:
Cryptocurrency prices dropped on Friday, reversing early gains on reports that crypto exchange Bybit was seeing $1.4 billion worth of crypto outflows in a potential security incident.

Bitcoin (BTC) slid to near $97,000 from challenging the $100,000 earlier during the day, while ether (ETH) slipped nearly 4%, dipping below $2,700.

The move happened after well-followed blockchain sleuth ZachXBT noted a "suspicious" $1.4 billion ETH outflow. Ben Zhou, CEO of Bybit confirmed the hack in an X post.

Read more: Bitcoin and Ether Tumble on Potential $1.46B Bybit Hack
